chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 993 of 2020 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of main case 10.12.2024 Mr. Ghulam Abbas Kuber, Assistant AG Sindh along with Abdul Sami, Assistant on behalf of Assistant Director, Local Government, Naushahro Feroze (respondent No.7) Mr. Zia-ul Haque Kamboh, Advocate for Town Committee, Mehrabpur >>>>>..<<<<< None present for the petitioner. No intimation is furnished. Aftab Mallah, Town Officer, Town Committee, Mehrabpur is present and files parawise comments on behalf of respondent No.7, taken on record. Learned counsel for the respondents as well Officer present on behalf of Town Officer, Town Committee, Mehrabpur submit that respondent No.8 is no more on the post as claimed by the petitioner, hence purpose of filing instant petition in view of the prayer clause has been achieved, therefore, it has become infructuous. Learned AAG is also of the same view. It appears that petitioner presented instant petition in the office on 29.09.2020 and then it was posed before the Court on 06.10.2020 when notices were ordered to be issued to other side and right from 06.10.2020 to date about four years have been passed neither petitioner nor his Counsel have taken any efforts to get listed the matter before the Court for hearing by filing any application. Such lethargic attitude on their part shows that either the purpose of filing instant petition has been achieved or the petitioner has lost his interest in proceeding it so he as well his Counsel have chosen to remain absent instead of pursuing the petition.

It appears that petitioner presented instant petition in the office on 29.09.2020 and then it was posed before the Court on 06.10.2020 when notices were ordered to be issued to other side and right from 06.10.2020 to date about four years have been passed neither petitioner nor his Counsel have taken any efforts to get listed the matter before the Court for hearing by filing any application. Such lethargic attitude on their part shows that either the purpose of filing instant petition has been achieved or the petitioner has lost his interest in proceeding it so he as well his Counsel have chosen to remain absent instead of pursuing the petition. In view of the comments as well statement advanced at the bar by counsel for the respondents, the petition in hand has become infructuous. Consequently, it is hereby dismissed. Judge Judge ARBROHI
